patch.value:
## Context

`pm history-compact` (src/cli/commands/history-compact.ts) is a complete, well-tested manual command. However, `PmSettings` has no `history.compact_policy` or `history.max_entries_before_compact` field. The `history` settings block only contains `missing_stream` (src/types.ts:645-647).

`pm stats --storage` now exposes the exact data needed to trigger compaction (largest_by_bytes, deepest_by_lines). The health check could surface a warning when a stream exceeds a configurable threshold.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add `history.compact_policy` settings block: `{ enabled: bool, max_entries: number, trigger: 'health_warn' | 'auto' }`
- [ ] `pm health` warns when any stream exceeds `max_entries` (trigger = health_warn)
- [ ] Optional: `pm health --fix` or a dedicated command auto-compacts the over-threshold streams
- [ ] `pm stats --storage` includes per-stream entry count in the largest-by-lines list
- [ ] Settings validator covers new fields; contract tests updated

## Evidence

- `src/types.ts:645-647` â€” history settings block has only `missing_stream`
- `src/core/history/history-storage-stats.ts:20-28` â€” `HistoryStorageStats` already collects `deepest_by_lines`
- `src/cli/commands/history-compact.ts:33-77` â€” compaction logic is complete but entirely manual
- `node dist/cli.js stats --storage` shows pm-th6y at 686 entries, 19.5MB total corpus
Auto-compact policy: config-driven threshold to trigger pm history-compact automatically

pm history-compact exists but is purely manual. There is no config-driven policy to auto-compact streams that exceed an entry count or byte threshold. Large projects accumulate streams like pm-th6y (686 entries, >19MB total corpus) with no automatic relief. Operators must discover and invoke compaction by hand.

- Add `history.compact_policy` settings block: `{ enabled: bool, max_entries: number, trigger: 'health_warn' | 'auto' }`
- `pm health` warns when any stream exceeds `max_entries` (trigger = health_warn)
- Optional: `pm health --fix` or a dedicated command auto-compacts the over-threshold streams
- `pm stats --storage` includes per-stream entry count in the largest-by-lines list
- Settings validator covers new fields; contract tests updated
